title: lycopersicon esculentum subsp. pimpinellifolium
intro: Lycopersicon esculentum subsp. pimpinellifolium, also known as the wild cherry tomato or the Campbell soup tomato's ancestor, is a subspecies of the tomato plant that is native to South America. The medicinal properties of this plant include its high antioxidant content, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of consuming Lycopersicon esculentum subsp. pimpinellifolium include reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain types of cancer, as well as improving immune function and digestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed for its medicinal properties, and it can also be used in tinctures and salves for topical application.
